name: Brandied Chocolate Truffles
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy medium saucepan, bring whipping cream and unsalted butter to a simmer.
2. Reduce heat to low and add the chopped bittersweet chocolate. Stir until melted.
3. Mix in the Cognac. Allow the mixture to stand at room temperature until firm enough to mound on a spoon (about 3-1/2 hours).
4. Line a cookie sheet with foil.
5. Spoon truffle mixture by rounded tablespoons onto the prepared cookie sheet, spacing apart.
6. Chill until firm for about 1 hour.
7. Roll each truffle between palms of hands into a ball and place on the same sheet.
8. Repeat with remaining truffles; refrigerate.
9. Melt the chopped bittersweet chocolate in the top of a double boiler over simmering water, stirring frequently until smooth. Remove from heat.
10. Dip each truffle into the melted chocolate, coating completely. Shake gently to remove excess chocolate.
11. Place the dipped truffles on the same sheet and refrigerate until firm (about 30 minutes).
12. Remove truffles from foil. They can be prepared up to one week ahead and stored in an airtight container. Serve cold.
